examples.en: He already went there and came back
He is going home
What did he do, did he leave for home?

words.etymology: From Old Georgian არს (ars), from Proto-Kartvelian *r-, *ar- (“to be”).

glosses: to write down or add a punctuation mark, to punctuate (on physical paper or electronic document)

words.etymology: The verbal meaning comes directly from Old Georgian უნდა, which used to be the imperfect, thus the -დ- screeve marker, the modern form created required screeves (უნდოდა, უნდოდეს).

glosses: to read (to look at and interpret letters or other information that is written)
to read (to speak aloud words or other information that is written)

words.etymology: From Old Georgian წერს (c̣ers). See further etymology on წერა (c̣era).

glosses: to remove, to take out, to extract
to see (to perceive with the eyes, or as if by sight)
to see, to notice (to detect with the eyes)

examples.en: Giorgi is studying.
Giorgi is learning his lessons.

glosses: to convene, to convoke, to call
to arouse, to call forth, to cause, to give rise to (to induce)
to seek out, to search out

examples.en: Let's dance together.

words.en: to begin, to start, to initiate
glosses: to begin, to start, to initiate

words.en: to be able to, to manage
glosses: to be able to, to manage

examples.ka: ჩამოვალ ახლავე, მოიცადე. მეორე სართულზე ვარ.
examples.roman: čamoval axlave, moicade. meore sartulze var.
examples.en: I'll come down in a second, wait. I'm on the 2nd floor.

glosses: to climb (a ladder, a mountain etc.)

examples.en: I just came out of the house.

glosses: to leave (to refrain from taking (something) away)

words.en: to lie (to rest in a horizontal position on a surface)
glosses: to lie (to rest in a horizontal position on a surface)
to mend, to repair, to fix
to straighten (to cause to become straight)
to correct, to rectify, to straighten out

words.en: to roll up (sleeves or trouser legs)
glosses: to roll up (sleeves or trouser legs)

words.en: to lay out, to put down (to place multiple objects onto a surface; e.g. laying books out on a table upon arrival)
glosses: to lay out, to put down (to place multiple objects onto a surface; e.g. laying books out on a table upon arrival)
to arrange, to sort (to set objects into a specific sequence or orderly arrangement; e.g. alphabetizing files)
to tidy, to clean up (to put a space in order; e.g. tidying a room or house)
to clear, to clear away (to pick things "up" and remove them from a surface; most commonly used for clearing a table)
to pack, to pack away (to put things "into" a container, suitcase, or bag)

words.en: to roll or lift up one’s something (sleeve, trouser legs, coat-tails, etc.)
glosses: to roll or lift up one’s something (sleeve, trouser legs, coat-tails, etc.)
to tolerate, to put up with
to lead out; to let pass

examples.en: When will you come (up) to the village?
The sun rose and lit up everything.

words.en: to complete, to end, to finish
glosses: to complete, to end, to finish
to perform, to execute, to carry out (:task)

words.en: to list, to scribe into a list
glosses: to list, to scribe into a list
to celebrate, to hold (:celebration, feast, etc.)
to spread (an idea, fear, hatred etc.)

words.etymology: From Old Georgian კუეთს (ḳuets), from Proto-Kartvelian *ḳwet-. Cognate with Mingrelian კვათუა (ḳvatua, “cutting”), Laz ოკვათუ (oǩvatu, “cutting”) and Svan ლიკუთურე (liḳuture, “cutting in thin pieces”).
From earlier *ცხობა (*cxoba). Compare ცხელი (cxeli), სიცხე (sicxe), etc.

glosses: to act in a disturbing manner
to issue (to deliver by authority: law, etc.)
to close, to shut (to remove or block an opening, gap or passage through)
to shut off (to stop or turn off by closing something, such as a valve)
to close, to shut (to put out of use or operation)
to assign (to designate or set apart (something) for some purpose)
to propose to (to ask for a person's hand in marriage)
to ring (to make (a bell, etc.) produce a resonant sound)
to ring (of a bell, etc., to produce a resonant sound)
to ring (to produce the sound of a bell or a similar sound)

examples.en: I will ring the bell.
I rang the bell.
The bell rang.
What a good song she sang!
I wish you a merry Christmas.
I wish you a merry Christmas.
Good luck.

glosses: to fry, to cook (on a pan)

examples.ka: ყველაფერი დაგიწვავს! საერთოდ იცი საჭმლის გაკეთება?
examples.roman: q̇velaperi dagic̣vavs! saertod ici sač̣mlis gaḳeteba?
examples.en: You've burned everything! Do you even know how to cook?
My mom fried some potatoes for us.
I wrote the address on the envelope
Sign here

glosses: to let (someone) go, to let pass
to pass (:something through a device, mechanism, process, etc.)

examples.en: Gvanca caught black butterflies.
The police arrested five thieves.
Aleko will hold the ball in hands.
When did he leave the company?

examples.ka: მას იქაურობა დაუტოვებია მანამ, სანამ პოლიცია მივიდოდა.
examples.roman: mas ikauroba dauṭovebia manam, sanam ṗolicia mividoda.
examples.en: He's apparently left the surroundings before the police arrived.

glosses: to crib, to lock, to closet, to pen, to latch

words.en: to declare, to announce, to state
glosses: to declare, to announce, to state
to give birth to, to bring forth

examples.en: to start a fire

examples.ka: სიცხეში ადამიანის ორგანიზმი წყალს დიდი რაოდენობით ითხოვს
examples.roman: sicxeši adamianis organizmi c̣q̇als didi raodenobit itxovs
examples.en: In hot weather human body requires water in large quantities
I'm interested.
Turn off the TV and study already!

glosses: to declare (to assert or announce formally, officially, explicitly, or emphatically)

words.etymology: From მო- (mo-) + აყრის (aq̇ris). Equivalently, from მოყრის (moq̇ris) with insertion of ა- (a-) version marker.

glosses: to marry, to marry off (to arrange for the marriage of; to give away as wife or husband)
